target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened malformed plugin/tool/schema metadata boundaries so hostile or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, or treated as absent instead of crashing provider routing, runtime tool resolution, Gateway/MCP catalogs, Codex prompt/report paths, plugin SDK helpers, bundle MCP projection/materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, and Plugin SDK host-hook/HTTP/provider-like/agent-harness registration.
- Kept fixtures synthetic: tests and notes use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, `mockplugin_status`, `mockplugin_store`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest update guards agent harness registration: unreadable harness fields now diagnose and skip only malformed harnesses, local/global harness registries keep stable normalized metadata, class-backed harness methods keep private state, and frozen/plain-object harnesses keep own data state with normalized receivers.
- Recent updates also guard provider-like registrations and HTTP route registration.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action/bundle-MCP/Codex dynamic-tool/Plugin-SDK metadata can crash, widen decisions, or bypass schema quarantine before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, Gateway catalog rendering, MCP loopback handling, approval forwarding, manifest/capability auth gating, provider payload conversion, prompt-cache setup, Codex reporting/instruction generation, plugin SDK normalization, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registration, agent harness registration, agent-core tool lookup, cached plugin descriptor execution, static plugin tool registration, bundled capability runtime capture, bundle MCP catalog projection, bundle MCP catalog materialization, Codex dynamic-tool bridge setup, plugin-tools MCP execution, lifecycle/event host-hook registration, session scheduler/action host-hook registration, session extension/trusted policy host-hook registration, reload/node host/node invoke/security audit registration, service/gateway discovery service registration, or command registration completes.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`, local focused lint/format checks, local autoreview, synthetic/private-name scrub, and AWS Crabbox Linux `pnpm check:changed`. Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because `blacksmith auth login` is required.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Pushed branch head `61141975d3fea7fc56349ee006ec6a5da9be5a13`. The current stack was last rebased onto `origin/main` `4b9a80d8958ad6d0e880e638a9d7f83ebb898f24`; `origin/main` advanced to `7f09d6ae48c7997ba0af8de34e65f95259362e25` after focused proof, so I did not chase another rebase.
- Latest agent-harness focused proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/registry.agent-harness.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Latest agent-harness focused format/diff checks: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/registry.agent-harness.test.ts src/agents/harness/registry.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Latest agent-harness focused lint: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/registry.agent-harness.test.ts src/agents/harness/registry.ts`.
- Local review for the agent-harness slice: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`, rerun clean after fixing accepted class-instance/global/frozen/plain-object receiver findings and a type-contract finding.
- Recent provider-like focused proof: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/registry.provider-like.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- Recent HTTP route focused proof after rebase: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/http-registry.test.ts src/config/sessions.cache.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate from the prior stack: `CRABBOX_AWS_REGION=us-east-1 CRABBOX_CAPACITY_REGIONS=us-east-1,us-east-2,us-west-2,eu-west-1,eu-central-1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "corepack pnpm check:changed"`.
- Changed-file, spec, and PR-body scrub for the reported plugin name and private path patterns.

Evidence after fix:

- Latest agent-harness focused proof passed: 1 file, 5 tests passed.
- Agent-harness focused oxfmt check, `git diff --check`, and targeted oxlint passed for `src/plugins/registry.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.agent-harness.test.ts`, and `src/agents/harness/registry.ts`.
- Autoreview for the agent-harness slice reran clean with no accepted/actionable findings after several real compatibility findings were fixed.
- Provider-like focused proof passed: 1 file, 9 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview after preserving provider instances.
- HTTP route focused proof passed after rebase: 2 files, 48 tests passed, with clean targeted lint/format and clean autoreview.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed on provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4fdce7317314`, run `run_f0ee5dfc90be`, against base `07870dff45eb` before later drift rebases.
- Current branch diff, local spec, and PR body scrub pass for the reported plugin name and private paths.

Observed result after fix:

- Synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures with unreadable names, schemas, descriptions, approval fields, provider metadata, provider-like ids, agent harness ids, MCP catalog fields, cached runtime descriptors, Codex dynamic-tool descriptors, MCP argument preparers, Plugin SDK tool metadata, Plugin SDK Control UI descriptors, runtime lifecycle fields, agent event subscription fields, scheduler job fields, session action fields, session extension fields, trusted policy fields, reload prefix fields, node host command fields, node invoke policy fields, service fields, gateway discovery service fields, command fields, and HTTP route fields no longer crash the relevant projection/resolution/registration paths.
- Healthy siblings continue through materialization, descriptor caching, provider payload conversion, MCP/Gateway listing, agent-core validation, Codex dynamic-tool bridge registration, plugin-tools MCP execution, plugin host-hook registration, HTTP route registration, provider-like registration, and agent harness registration while malformed siblings are omitted, quarantined, or diagnosed.
- Agent harness registration now exposes stable normalized metadata while preserving runtime method receivers for class-backed and plain-object harnesses.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because `blacksmith` is not authenticated in this shell.
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed` was not rerun after the latest HTTP route, provider-like, and agent-harness slices; the previous broad pass was on the earlier stack, and the latest slices have focused proof, formatting, targeted oxlint, and autoreview.
- GitHub CI for the newest push is still running/pending.
- No live third-party plugin was referenced or exercised; tests intentionally use synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`mockplugin` fixtures.

## Current review state

- PR remains draft while this fuzzing/hardening lane continues.
- Latest pushed commit: `61141975d3fea7fc56349ee006ec6a5da9be5a13` (`fix(plugins): guard agent harness registration`).

target.title: Retry node shell fallback on ENOENT
target.body:
## Summary

- Retry node-host `system.run` shell execution with trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` when the default `/bin/sh` spawn fails with ENOENT.
- Keep PATH-resolved shell fallback out of the retry chain so execution does not switch to an inherited-PATH binary after approval analysis.
- Keep non-shell and Windows invocations unchanged.
- Add regression coverage for the runtime retry chain.

Fixes #85202

## Why

Linux headless node hosts can report `spawn /bin/sh ENOENT` even when the node is connected and `system.which`/manual host checks show a usable shell at `/usr/bin/sh`. The previous node-host path treated the first spawn error as terminal, so `exec host=node` failed before trying another trusted POSIX shell path.

## Verification

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/node-host/invoke.node-shell-fallback.test.ts src/infra/node-shell.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.test.ts src/node-host/invoke.sanitize-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed; 24 tests
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Node-host `system.run` no longer treats a missing default `/bin/sh` spawn as the only possible POSIX shell path; the patched node host retries trusted absolute `/usr/bin/sh` on ENOENT.

Real environment tested:
Linux VPS source checkout at `/root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback`, Node.js v22.22.2, patched OpenClaw node-host runtime code at PR head `fd0fdfb445`. I also ran the actual patched `src/node-host/invoke.ts` `runCommand` path inside a minimal Linux chroot proof root where `/bin/sh` is absent and `/usr/bin/sh -> dash` exists.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
First ran the focused runtime/test/type gates from the patched checkout. Then ran the actual patched node-host `runCommand` implementation in a Linux chroot with an empty `/bin` directory and `/usr/bin/sh` available:

```text
/usr/sbin/chroot /tmp/openclaw-node-shell-proof-root \
  /usr/bin/node --import /repo/node_modules/tsx/dist/esm/index.mjs \
  /repo/.tmp-node-shell-proof.mjs
```

The proof script called:

```ts
await runCommand(
  ["/bin/sh", "-lc", "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"],
  undefined,
  undefined,
  5000,
);
```

Evidence after fix:
Terminal output from the live chroot runtime proof:

```text
proof root /bin/sh exists: false
proof root /usr/bin/sh exists: true
proof root /usr/bin/sh target: dash
runCommand result: {
  "exitCode": 0,
  "timedOut": false,
  "success": true,
  "stdout": "NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK",
  "stderr": "",
  "error": null,
  "truncated": false
}
```

Focused test output from the patched checkout:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  13 passed (13)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  2 passed (2)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /root/projects/openclaw-node-shell-fallback

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 19.64s
```

Observed result after fix:
The actual patched node-host runtime path returned success from `/usr/bin/sh` with stdout `NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK` when invoked in a Linux root where `/bin/sh` was absent and `/usr/bin/sh` existed. The focused regression separately asserts the retry sequence:

```text
/bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
/usr/bin/sh -lc "printf NODE_SHELL_FALLBACK_OK"
```

What was not tested:
I still do not have the reporter's exact paired Linux headless node/systemd setup connected to this gateway, so this is runtime proof of the patched node-host command path under the same missing-`/bin/sh` failure condition, not a full paired-node `exec host=node` end-to-end recording from the original environment.

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The repair is **layered on top of the existing scanner** — all current scanner logic is preserved:

1. `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` — identifies stale bundled runtime skill paths
2. `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` — computes correct expected path with `pathExists` validation
3. `loadSessionStoreForSnapshotScan` — hydrates `skillsSnapshot.promptRef` blobbed values before scanning
4. `collectResolvedSkillPaths` — scans both `filePath` and `baseDir` fields
5. `collectCachedSnapshotPaths` — covers `skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills`, and `injectedWorkspaceFiles`

The only additions are:
- `replacePathsInSession()` — scoped replacement that modifies only snapshot metadata fields
- `shouldRepair?: boolean` parameter on `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()`
- Repair block that reuses scanner findings and applies replacements
- `.bak` backup + `writeTextAtomic` for safe writes

**Key design: scoped replacement** — the repair parses `sessions.json`, finds the specific session by key, and replaces paths only in the affected fields (`skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills[].filePath/baseDir`, `injectedWorkspaceFiles[].path`). Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is never modified.

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +168 lines (replacePathsInSession + repair block)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script (6 scenarios)

## Path Replacement Strategy

The repair handles three forms of stale paths in sessions.json:

1. **JSON-escaped** (Windows backslashes, unicode): `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` generates the escaped form
2. **XML-escaped** (skillsSnapshot.prompt locations): `&` `<` `>` `"` `'` entity encoding
3. **Raw unescaped** (fallback for non-encoded storage)

**Scoped replacement:** Only modifies the specific session's snapshot metadata fields. Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes), XML-escaped paths (`&` in skillsSnapshot.prompt), and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills (both POSIX and Windows separators). **Critical:** The repair must be scoped to snapshot metadata fields only, not modify unrelated session content (transcripts, user messages). The repair must preserve the existing scanner's safeguards: promptRef hydration, `pathExists` validation, `filePath`/`baseDir` scanning, and `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` logic.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Live production instance with 14 sessions affected by pnpm runtime root change (see Issue #85689 for live output). Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from the PR, using the same `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` pattern as current main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**1. Live production instance (from Issue #85689):**

```
Session snapshots
- Found 14 sessions with stale cached session metadata paths.
  Live bundled skills root is healthy:
  ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  Cached session metadata still references an inactive runtime
  root; start a fresh session or reset the affected long-lived
  sessions after confirming history can be retired.
  Store: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  - agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_bc71ec62b088ed55e66fcc23c18a0056
    skillsSnapshot.prompt:
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20_@ty
  pes+express@5.0.6/.../clawhub/SKILL.md
    ->
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  nclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md
  ...and 209 more stale cached paths.
```

**2. Standalone reproduction script output (6 scenarios):**

```
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Real Behavior Proof: Doctor Session Snapshot Auto-Repair
Date: 2026-05-29T03:40:51.634Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Scenario 1: WITHOUT repair — findings computed but not applied
  Findings computed: 2 stale paths
  Repair performed: NO (shouldRepair not enabled)
  PASS

Scenario 2: WITH repair — paths replaced correctly
  Paths replaced: 2
  Backup created: true
  JSON valid: true
  Stale paths remaining: 0
  PASS

Scenario 3: Windows backslash paths — JSON-escaped in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 4: XML-escaped prompt paths — entity-encoded in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 5: Idempotent — second run finds nothing
  Second scan findings: 0
  PASS

Scenario 6: Scoped replacement — unrelated content preserved
  Paths replaced: 2
  Transcript preserved: true
  User message preserved: true
  Prompt repaired: true
  Other session repaired: true
  PASS

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Results: 6 passed, 0 failed

  The repair uses the existing scanner (extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments
  + resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath) to compute expected paths, then applies
  scoped replacement to only modify snapshot metadata fields.
  Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages) is preserved unchanged.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 6 scenarios pass. The live production instance (Issue #85689) shows the exact real-world scenario this PR addresses: 14 sessions with 223 stale paths after pnpm runtime root change. The repair correctly handles POSIX paths, Windows backslash paths (JSON-escaped), XML-escaped entity paths in skillsSnapshot.prompt (using paths with `&` character), and baseDir directory paths (both POSIX `/SKILL.md` and Windows `\\SKILL.md`). The repair is idempotent (Scenario 5). **Critical: Scoped replacement (Scenario 6) proves that only snapshot metadata fields are modified — transcripts and user messages containing the same stale paths are preserved unchanged.** JSON validity is preserved after rewriting. Writes use `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe atomic operations.

**What was not tested:**
- Live `openclaw doctor --fix` on the affected instance (requires building from PR branch — local build fails due to missing native bindings in container environment, documented in AGENTS.md)
- Concurrent access to sessions.json during repair (single-threaded doctor execution)

**Proof coverage:**
The standalone script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`:
- **Reuses current main's scanner pattern**: `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` for expected path computation
- Scoped replacement: parse JSON, find session by key, modify only snapshot metadata fields
- `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` for JSON-escaped path generation
- XML entity escaping for `&` `<` `>` `"` `'`
- Uses paths with actual XML-sensitive characters (`&` in `my&company`)
- Try JSON-escaped form first, then XML-escaped, then raw as fallback
- Handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, special characters
- Backup creation before write
- JSON validity preservation after repair
- Uses `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe writes
- **Scenario 6: Unrelated content preservation** — proves transcript and user message fields are not modified

commands.review_summary:

target.title: fix(imessage): avoid threaded sms approval updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Prevents iMessage SMS approval result updates from being sent as threaded replies to the original approval prompt.
- Keeps normal iMessage and chat-target approval updates threaded, preserving existing behavior outside SMS handles.
- Isolates the SMS fix to the iMessage approval handler; broader native approval route-gate refactors and inbound delivery targeting changes are intentionally out of scope.
- Review should focus on whether SMS handle targets should omit `replyToId` while all other iMessage targets continue to include it.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #87770

Requested by maintainer in the PR isolation workflow.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: SMS approval updates should preserve SMS delivery behavior instead of sending result messages as threaded iMessage replies.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS; branch created from current `origin/main` and pushed to `origin/codex/imessage-sms-approval-fix`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`; `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Branch diff is limited to `extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.
- Observed result after fix: SMS handle targets no longer pass `replyToId` when sending approval update messages; non-SMS iMessage targets still pass `replyToId`.
- What was not tested: Live iMessage/SMS approval flow was not rerun on this isolated branch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This PR intentionally isolates the code change and relies on CI plus focused diff proof; no live phone-number proof is included in the PR body.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, `updateEntry` always passed `replyToId: entry.messageId` for all iMessage approval targets, including `sms:` handles.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --stat origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD`

Regression coverage added or updated: None; this is a narrow runtime routing change isolated from the broader native approval helper refactor.

What failed before this fix, if known: SMS approval update delivery was mixed with threaded iMessage reply behavior because `replyToId` was passed unconditionally.

If no test was added, why not? The change is isolated to one target-preparation branch and is being split out from the broader PR for review; CI will run the standard gates.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. SMS approval result messages should no longer be sent as threaded replies.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The iMessage approval update path for SMS handle targets.

How is that risk mitigated?

The condition only suppresses `replyToId` for parsed `sms:` handle targets; parse failures and non-SMS targets retain the previous threaded behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Run CI and review the isolated iMessage approval-handler diff.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and any maintainer-requested live iMessage proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet on this isolated PR.

target.title: fix(logging): refresh file log hostname per write
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #87258.
- Resolve file-log hostnames lazily for each JSONL write instead of freezing the module-load value.
- Keep the `"unknown"` fallback for transient empty hostnames, but allow later writes to recover once `os.hostname()` returns a real value.
- Keep top-level `hostname` and tslog `_meta.hostname` aligned from the same resolved value.
- Add regression coverage for an initial empty hostname followed by a later real hostname.

## Verification

- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/logger-redaction-behavior.test.ts src/logger.test.ts`
- PASS: `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/logging/logger.ts src/logging/logger-redaction-behavior.test.ts`
- PASS: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- PASS: `OPENCLAW_TEST_FILE_LOG=1 node --import tsx/esm -e 'const fs = await import("node:fs"); const os = await import("node:os"); const path = await import("node:path"); const { getLogger, setLoggerOverride, resetLogger } = await import("./src/logging.js"); const logPath = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `openclaw-log-proof-${process.pid}.jsonl`); setLoggerOverride({ level: "info", file: logPath }); getLogger().info({ route: "/" }, "hello"); resetLogger(); const record = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(logPath, "utf8")); fs.unlinkSync(logPath); console.log(JSON.stringify({ top: record.hostname, meta: record._meta?.hostname, message: record.message }));'`
- PASS: `codex review --uncommitted` after the `_meta.hostname` alignment fix reported no correctness issues.
- UNRELATED CURRENT BASE FAILURE: `pnpm check:changed` fails in `typecheck core tests` on `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts` with `Type '{ pattern: string; }[]' is not assignable to type 'never[]'`; that file is unchanged from `origin/main` (`git diff --exit-code origin/main -- src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: JSONL file logs no longer permanently report `_meta.hostname: "unknown"` when the first hostname lookup during module startup is empty.

Real environment tested: macOS local checkout on `fix/87258-lazy-log-hostname`, using the file JSONL transport with `OPENCLAW_TEST_FILE_LOG=1`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_TEST_FILE_LOG=1 node --import tsx/esm -e 'const fs = await import("node:fs"); const os = await import("node:os"); const path = await import("node:path"); const { getLogger, setLoggerOverride, resetLogger } = await import("./src/logging.js"); const logPath = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `openclaw-log-proof-${process.pid}.jsonl`); setLoggerOverride({ level: "info", file: logPath }); getLogger().info({ route: "/" }, "hello"); resetLogger(); const record = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(logPath, "utf8")); fs.unlinkSync(logPath); console.log(JSON.stringify({ top: record.hostname, meta: record._meta?.hostname, message: record.message }));'`

Evidence after fix: the live JSONL proof printed `{"top":"Shubhankars-MacBook-Pro.local","meta":"Shubhankars-MacBook-Pro.local","message":"hello"}`. The regression test also simulates `os.hostname()` returning `""` for the first write and `"lr-macbook"` for the second write.

Observed result after fix: the first empty hostname write keeps the safe `"unknown"` fallback, and later JSONL writes re-resolve the real hostname into both top-level `hostname` and `_meta.hostname`.

What was not tested: a separate long-running Gateway restart reproducing the intermittent macOS startup timing; the source-level race is covered by the stubbed resolver test plus the live file-log write proof.

target.title: fix(codex): prevent false completion stalls during native streams
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep raw assistant progress on the documented `turn/completed` terminal guard; progress text is still not treated as a final answer.
- Count forwarded native Codex response stream deltas, including `response.custom_tool_call_input.delta`, as active turn activity and attempt progress while a turn is already open.
- Guard unscoped native `response.*.delta` notifications so they only refresh a turn when the shared app-server client has one active lease; concurrent leased turns no longer keep each other's watchdogs alive.
- Keep the post-tool raw-assistant fallback timeout conservative at `5 * 60_000` / `300000` ms for app-server/native paths that do not forward stream deltas, and align runtime docs plus plugin metadata.
- Preserve recovery behavior for actual completion stalls: replay only replay-safe attempts; for side-effecting/non-replay-safe stalls retire the stuck app-server client, clear stale native thread binding only when it still points at the timed-out thread, release the lane, and surface a recoverable incomplete-turn result.
- After the one replay-safe stdio retry is exhausted, a repeated `turn_completion_idle_timeout` now falls through to the normal timeout payload path instead of rejecting the embedded run.

## Why this shape
The failure is not that raw assistant progress needs to become deliverable final text. The failure is that native Codex can spend a long time actively streaming a large tool input/patch after a raw assistant progress item has completed, before any completed tool-call notification exists. Without counting those `response.*.delta` events as activity, OpenClaw can falsely fire `turn.completion_idle_timeout` while the model is still working.

This keeps the lifecycle semantics strict: final delivery still requires `turn/completed`; active native stream deltas only keep the watchdog from killing a live turn. The latest review fixes also avoid cross-talk for unscoped deltas and keep retry-exhausted completion-idle failures on the timeout-result path.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: Codex-backed Telegram turns could be falsely aborted while native Codex was still streaming response deltas for a large tool input/patch after raw assistant progress, producing `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. Some work may already have been performed; verify the current state before retrying.`
- Real environment tested: Keshav's live OpenClaw gateway and Telegram group flow on 2026-05-29 IST.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav built and restarted the gateway from this PR branch after the native-delta runtime fix, then reattempted the Vietnam webpage edit flow that had repeatedly failed with the completion-idle watchdog. The latest pushed head `2aa61b24ea` is rebased onto `origin/main` `040eba1cdc`; the final commit on top is test-only stabilization after the runtime proof.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the Telegram operator thread:

```text
2026-05-29 21:46 IST, Keshav G:
I built and restarted server
Then reattempted the vietnam webpage and yay it worked this time without errors

2026-05-29 23:41 IST, Keshav G:
I have build and restarted
Now do the tests, ci and submit latest proof from latest Heads
```

- Observed result after fix: The previously repeated Vietnam webpage edit flow completed without the prior `turn.completion_idle_timeout` / `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete` error after the gateway was built and restarted from the PR branch.
- What was not tested: The bot did not personally restart the live gateway; Keshav performed the build/restart. Latest GitHub Actions were still running when this proof was added, and local validation is listed below.

## Review feedback addressed
Latest head: `2aa61b24ea` on `origin/main` base `040eba1cdc`.

- Plugin config metadata and both Codex harness docs now agree with the runtime default: `postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` defaults to `300000` ms.
- Timeout cleanup now clears the persisted Codex app-server binding only if the stored binding still points at the timed-out thread, preserving transient-turn bindings.
- Unscoped native response deltas now count as activity only when the shared app-server client reports a single active lease; scoped deltas for the active turn still count directly.
- Retry-exhausted replay-safe stdio `turn_completion_idle_timeout` failures now surface the timeout payload instead of throwing the prompt error after the one retry has already been used.
- Added focused regression coverage for binding-preserving cleanup, concurrent-lease unscoped deltas, native response delta activity, and retry-exhausted completion-idle timeout handling.

## Tests / validation
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed on head `2aa61b24ea`.
- Targeted `oxfmt --check` passed on changed TypeScript/plugin files on head `2aa61b24ea`.
- Focused Vitest passed on head `2aa61b24ea`: `6` files / `82` tests.

Focused Vitest command:

```bash
./node_modules/.bin/vitest run \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-results.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts \
  extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts \
  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts \
  --reporter=dot
```

## Related issue
- Related to #87744. This PR addresses the Codex-backed Telegram completion-idle family; the live proof narrows one concrete false-timeout cause to native response delta streams not being counted as turn activity.

target.title: refactor: share native approval route gates
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `createNativeApprovalChannelRouteGates` to the public plugin SDK native approval runtime surface.
- Converts WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage native approval routing to the shared route-gate helper while keeping channel-specific target normalization and transport/account lookup local.
- Updates SDK docs and refreshes the plugin SDK API baseline for the new exported helper.
- Keeps native delivery, shared forwarding fallback suppression, and local `/approve` fallback responsibilities separate.

## Linked context

Related #87413

Requested by maintainer via Codex implementation spec for native approval channel routes.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Native approval channels duplicated the same exec/plugin approval forwarding route-gate logic. This change centralizes the shared approval-family, mode, filter, account-binding, session-target, and explicit-target checks in the plugin SDK and adopts it in WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS prod Pi gateway from the PR worktree, WhatsApp desktop app, Signal sender account, and iMessage through the approved Google Voice SMS thread.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch`; `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json" node dist/index.js gateway --port 18789`; live WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage approval rows for exec/plugin approve and deny; `node .mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/scripts/validate-artifacts.mjs`; `uvx showboat verify .mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/raw/showboat-summary.md`; `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Durable Showboat proof at `.mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/raw/showboat-summary.md` verifies the full approval matrix. The validator reports pass for setup, WhatsApp plugin approve/deny, WhatsApp exec approve/deny, Signal plugin approve/deny, Signal exec approve/deny, iMessage plugin approve/deny, iMessage exec approve/deny, video blocker artifact, and no `src/infra/*` diff. iMessage screenshot proof is saved under `.mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/raw/imessage-exec-deny-2613-messages-proof.png`.
- Observed result after fix: WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage/Google Voice each created one live approval request for exec approve, exec deny, plugin approve, and plugin deny. `/approve ... allow-once` resolved approve rows and produced the expected success/file-created result. `/approve ... deny` resolved deny rows and blocked the plugin or left the requested `/tmp` file absent.
- What was not tested: Unit tests were not rerun for the latest live-proof fixes per maintainer direction for this pass.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The iMessage native request/followup send path logged `imsg rpc timeout (send)` in some rows even though the Google Voice surface showed the request or decision and the gateway resolved the approval. Video capture was skipped to avoid recording unrelated private messaging content; `raw/video-blocker.md` records that blocker.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The replaced channel files each carried local copies of approval forwarding config selection, mode checks, filter matching, account target matching, and session/target route checks.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/build-all.mjs gatewayWatch`
- Live prod gateway on port 18789 with `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=prod` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json"`
- Live WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage/Google Voice approval rows for plugin approve, plugin deny, exec approve, and exec deny
- `node .mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/scripts/validate-artifacts.mjs`
- `uvx showboat verify .mem/main/proofs/demo-35-prod-approval-matrix-whatsapp-signal-imessage/raw/showboat-summary.md`
- `git diff --check`
- No unit tests were run for the latest live-proof fixes per maintainer direction.

Regression coverage already present in `src/plugin-sdk/approval-native-helpers.test.ts` covers exec/plugin family separation, `nativeSessionOnly`, filters, default-account fallback, single-enabled-account fallback, and explicit target eligibility. Existing WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage native approval tests cover the adopted channel behavior.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Native approval-capable channels now share one SDK route-gate implementation for the same intended approval forwarding decisions.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. This does not add or migrate config keys; it reuses existing `approvals.exec` and `approvals.plugin` config.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Approval routing affects where exec/plugin approval prompts are eligible to appear, so the highest-risk area is suppressing a fallback prompt when native delivery cannot actually own the same route.

What is the highest-risk area?

Forwarding fallback suppression and account-scoped target matching across session and explicit-target modes.

How is that risk mitigated?

The helper preserves the previous per-channel checks, adds direct helper tests for family/mode/filter/account behavior, keeps target-only shared fallback delivery available when native session handling is not eligible, and passes existing channel-specific fallback suppression tests plus Slack QA lab native approval scenarios.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review. CI is pending after PR creation.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas.
- Include two small current-main gate unblockers hit by this changed lane: type the exec approval mock allowlist result, and remove a redundant `unknown | undefined` schema-cache annotation.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts --reporter=dot` (84 tests passed after final rebase on `82f8ba9a474`)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/openai-tool-schema.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean, no accepted/actionable findings after final rebase)
- AWS Crabbox `check:changed`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4605e66e6f2f`, run `run_e94bc87a3cea`, base `b7e9272dbec`, head `013fb59dc0e`: exit 0. Final branch was then cleanly rebased to `82f8ba9a474` on `31169ff3b43`; focused tests, diff check, file-level oxlint, and autoreview were rerun after that rebase.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree plus AWS Crabbox changed gate through `core`, `coreTests`, and `docs` lanes.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, including assertions that `text_delta` accounting does not call `partial.toJSON` and that opaque diagnostic chunks do not break stream delivery; Crabbox run `run_e94bc87a3cea` completed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.
Observed result after fix: `responseStreamBytes` is computed from incremental delta bytes for high-frequency stream deltas and from snapshotless chunks for other partial-bearing stream events.
What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session; the fix is validated through the diagnostics stream accounting path and changed-gate proof.

target.title: Memory/QMD: isolate mcporter sidecars per agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate QMD mcporter daemon/config state per agent by writing an agent-scoped mcporter config and passing it to mcporter calls.
- Preserve the configured mcporter server definition instead of replacing custom/remote server config with a hard-coded local `qmd mcp` entry.
- Keep QMD model/env settings flowing only through the generated stdio server entry, while stripping QMD-scoped XDG env from mcporter's own process environment.
- Tolerate mcporter daemon log lines before JSON responses so warm daemon calls do not break memory search parsing.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** QMD memory search through mcporter must keep each agent on its own mcporter config/daemon state while preserving the configured `qmd` mcporter server and avoiding QMD-scoped XDG env leakage into mcporter's own process env.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with Node `v24.15.0`, `mcporter 0.10.2`, `qmd 2.1.0`, configured agents `hex` and `trump`, and the configured mcporter `qmd` stdio server. Home paths are redacted as `~`; private snippets are omitted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
node --version
mcporter --version
qmd --version
mcporter config get qmd --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
node -e '<read generated ~/.openclaw/agents/{hex,trump}/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json and print redacted command/env summary>'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output, redacted:

```text
node --version -> v24.15.0
mcporter --version -> 0.10.2
qmd --version -> qmd 2.1.0

mcporter config get qmd --json
{
  "name": "qmd",
  "source": { "kind": "local", "path": "~/.mcporter/mcporter.json" },
  "transport": "stdio",
  "command": "~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd",
  "args": ["mcp"]
}

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent hex --json
  agentId=hex backend=qmd files=1310 chunks=1310 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm openclaw memory status --deep --agent trump --json
  agentId=trump backend=qmd files=204 chunks=204 dirty=false vector.available=true vector.semanticAvailable=true embeddingProbe.ok=true collections=4

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent hex --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=memory; first.score=0.93

pnpm --silent openclaw memory search --agent trump --json --max-results 1 "OpenClaw"
  exited 0; results.length=1; first.source=sessions; first.score=0.93

Generated mcporter config summaries:
hex:   path=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json   imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/hex/qmd/xdg-cache
trump: path=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/mcporter/mcporter.json imports=[] command=~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/qmd args=["mcp"] XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config QMD_CONFIG_DIR=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-config/qmd XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.openclaw/agents/trump/qmd/xdg-cache
```

**Observed result after fix:** Both agents reported healthy QMD vector readiness (`embeddingProbe.ok=true`, `vector.available=true`, `vector.semanticAvailable=true`), both `memory search` commands returned one result successfully, and the generated mcporter configs were separate per agent while preserving the configured `qmd` stdio command.

**What was not tested:** I did not test a live remote HTTP mcporter server; that preservation path is covered by the focused unit test added in this PR.

target.title: perf(test): fix explicit-file Vitest wrapper hangs
target.body:
## Summary

- Route simple explicit-file `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` invocations through the existing `scripts/test-projects.mjs` project router so single-file agent proof does not load the full root Vitest project matrix.
- Keep direct Vitest behavior for explicit config/root/project, watch/dev mode, and separate option-value invocations where project-router parsing could change semantics.
- Add the missing active-memory and Codex extension project-router kinds so delegated explicit extension tests run real shards instead of being skipped.

## Timing Proof

| Case | Command | Result |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Before this patch, temporary copy of `HEAD:scripts/run-vitest.mjs` with a 5s idle watchdog | `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 node scripts/.run-vitest-before.$PID.mjs test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts` | No Vitest output before watchdog; SIGTERM at 5s, forced SIGKILL after another 5s; `/usr/bin/time -p` reported `real 13.92`, `user 13.02`, `sys 0.85`. |
| After this patch | `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts` | Selected `test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts`, 7 tests passed; `[test] passed ... in 2.36s`; `/usr/bin/time -p` reported `real 2.48`, `user 2.01`, `sys 0.29`. |
| Reported Minimax example after this patch | `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts` | Selected `test/vitest/vitest.extension-providers.config.ts`, 4 tests passed; `[test] passed ... in 4.26s`; `/usr/bin/time -p` reported `real 4.31`, `user 3.97`, `sys 1.11`. |
| Reported changelog attribution example after this patch | `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-changelog-attributions.test.ts` | Selected `test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts`, 8 tests passed; `[test] passed ... in 2.67s`; `/usr/bin/time -p` reported `real 2.72`, `user 2.33`, `sys 0.58`. |

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/run-vitest.test.ts` - 31 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` - 103 tests passed.
- `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts` - 7 tests passed; `real 2.48`.
- `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts` - 4 tests passed; `real 4.31`.
- `/usr/bin/time -p node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-changelog-attributions.test.ts` - 8 tests passed; `real 2.72`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/index.test.ts` - 7 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts` - 127 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/run-vitest.mjs scripts/test-projects.test-support.mjs test/scripts/run-vitest.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts` - passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Bare explicit-file `scripts/run-vitest.mjs <file>` agent runs no longer stall in the root multi-project Vitest startup path for the reported examples; they select the narrow project shard and run the requested file.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on branch `vitest-hanging`, Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.2.2.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.

Evidence after fix: The three reported examples completed and printed their selected shard. `test/scripts/dependency-guard-workflow.test.ts` improved from forced-kill at `real 13.92` with a 5s watchdog to a successful run at `real 2.48`.

Observed result after fix: The wrapper delegates simple explicit-file invocations to `test-projects.mjs`, preserves direct Vitest behavior for option-heavy/watch/config/root/project cases, and routes active-memory/Codex extension targets to real extension configs.

What was not tested: Full Vitest suite, full `pnpm check`, remote Testbox/Crabbox proof.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve Codex auth for compaction fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86820.

OpenAI compaction fallback now keeps the selected Codex runtime route when Codex runtime metadata is available, while keeping the configured provider identity for context-window and config lookup. This lets Codex OAuth sessions route compaction model loading through `openai-codex` instead of falling back to direct `openai` API-key auth.

This also keeps direct OpenAI API-key compaction unchanged when no Codex runtime provider is configured, and leaves custom OpenAI-compatible base URLs untouched.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

`pnpm check:changed` delegated to Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01kstn684tqa5zy6t2z03yv2wt`, but the delegated run stopped producing output after setup and was cancelled. The earlier delegated run `tbx_01kstmz93ndp9reg6ecw9y6508` caught two type errors; both were fixed and the local core typecheck above passed afterward.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex OAuth-backed OpenAI compaction fallback no longer drops the Codex runtime route and asks for direct `OPENAI_API_KEY` when `openai-codex` runtime provider metadata is present.

Real environment tested: Local source checkout with focused regression tests and core typecheck; no live Codex OAuth account flow was executed from this checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`; `git diff --check`; autoreview command above.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests cover Codex runtime OpenAI compaction without explicit Codex auth order, direct OpenAI API-key preservation when no Codex runtime provider is configured, OpenAI compaction model overrides through Codex runtime auth, custom OpenAI-compatible base URL preservation, and active direct compaction model loading through `openai-codex`.

Observed result after fix: Focused Vitest passed 5 files and 157 tests; core typecheck exited cleanly; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: A live installed OpenClaw gateway with real Codex OAuth compaction and no `OPENAI_API_KEY`; delegated `pnpm check:changed` hung after setup and was cancelled as noted above.

target.title: fix(auth): cooldown inline api key billing failures
target.body:
## Summary
- track provider-level usage/backoff state for inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials without creating synthetic auth profiles
- skip temporarily disabled inline API keys during auth resolution and availability checks
- mark inline API keys on provider auth/billing failures so repeated 402s do not immediately retry the same exhausted key

Closes #39807.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Inline `models.providers.<id>.apiKey` credentials now record provider auth/billing failures in usage state, stop immediate reuse during cooldown, and become available again after the cooldown expires without creating a synthetic auth profile.
- **Environment tested**: Local Linux workstation on PR branch `fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown` at `61ac6e93eac913fee891d3d63262518e08185c9d`, using Node/tsx against the production auth profile store, usage helpers, and model auth resolution helpers.
- **Steps run after the patch**: Ran a Node terminal probe that created a temporary agent directory, configured a dummy inline provider API key, resolved availability before failure, called `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)` with reason `billing`, checked availability/resolution during cooldown, then simulated cooldown expiry and resolved the inline key again.
- **Evidence**:

```text
[agent/embedded] auth profile failure state updated: runId=real-proof-inline-billing profile=sha256:f68577404b84 provider=demo-local reason=billing window=disabled reused=false
{
  "behavior": "inline provider apiKey billing failure disables only that inline key and blocks immediate reuse",
  "beforeFailure": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  },
  "recordedFailure": {
    "usageId": "inline-api-key:demo-local",
    "profileCreated": false,
    "disabledReason": "billing",
    "disabledUntilIsFuture": true
  },
  "duringCooldown": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": false,
    "blocked": true,
    "blockedMessage": "Inline API key for provider \"demo-local\" is temporarily disabled after a provider auth/billing failure. Retry after about 300 minutes, or switch to a different auth profile/API key."
  },
  "afterCooldownExpiry": {
    "hasAvailableAuth": true,
    "resolvedMode": "api-key",
    "resolvedSource": "models.json"
  }
}
```

- **Observed result**: Before the billing failure, the inline key is available and resolves from `models.json`. After `markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...)`, `usageStats["inline-api-key:demo-local"]` records `disabledReason: "billing"`, `disabledUntil` is in the future, no synthetic profile is created, `hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...)` returns false, and `resolveApiKeyForProvider(...)` blocks immediate reuse with the temporary-disabled error. After simulated cooldown expiry, the same inline key becomes available and resolves from `models.json` again.
- **Not tested**: A live paid provider 402 against a real provider account; this avoids intentionally causing a real billing failure while still exercising the production failure-marking and auth-resolution path with a redacted dummy inline key.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts` (2 files, 66 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Additional real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed after review**: the actual embedded-runner failure paths now call the inline provider API-key marker even when the active credential has no profile id.
- **Environment tested**: local checkout after merging upstream/main into fix/inline-api-key-billing-cooldown at ce378baab4.

## Follow-up proof after env-backed cooldown repair

- **Behavior addressed after ClawSweeper review**: models.providers.<id>.apiKey credentials backed by env markers or env SecretRefs now use the same inline provider cooldown gate as literal inline keys. Provider availability also reports env-backed inline keys unavailable while the inline cooldown window is active.
- **Availability/cache behavior**: markInlineProviderApiKeyFailure(...) now fires the same auth failure hook used by profile failures, so warmed provider auth state is cleared and recomputed after inline-key billing/auth failures instead of continuing to advertise stale availability.
- **Regression coverage added**:
  - configured env-marker apiKey is blocked during inline provider cooldown

  - hasAvailableAuthForProvider(...) returns false for those cooled-down env-backed inline keys

## Summary

- Replaces #85723 with core-owned session goals instead of a plugin/lease extension surface.
- Persists goals on `SessionEntry`, exposes `get_goal`/`create_goal`/`update_goal` model tools, and adds native/text `/goal` controls.
- Adds resumable blocked/budget-limited goal behavior, projected session-row goal state, and a compact TUI footer goal indicator.
- Adds a dedicated Goal docs page plus TUI/slash-command cross-links and focused regression coverage.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: Core-owned thread/session goals with model tools, `/goal` controls, TUI footer status, and dedicated docs, replacing plugin-owned PR #85723.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...` on touched TS/docs files
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` on touched TS files
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`

Evidence after fix: Formatting, oxlint, docs index, docs MDX, docs formatting, docs links, whitespace, direct TS smokes, and final local autoreview passed. Final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: Goals persist in core session state; model tools can read/create/complete/block goals; `/goal` can start, show, pause, resume, complete, block, and clear goals; blocked goals can be resumed; budget-limited goals resume with a fresh budget window; stale token baselines are not overcounted; TUI session rows carry goal state and the footer shows compact goal status.

What was not tested: Vitest wrapper did not complete in this checkout; it hung without test output and was stopped. Earlier broad `tsgo` was blocked on existing unrelated `src/config/io.ts` unique-symbol errors.

target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The repair is **layered on top of the existing scanner** — all current scanner logic is preserved:

1. `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` — identifies stale bundled runtime skill paths
2. `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` — computes correct expected path with `pathExists` validation
3. `loadSessionStoreForSnapshotScan` — hydrates `skillsSnapshot.promptRef` blobbed values before scanning
4. `collectResolvedSkillPaths` — scans both `filePath` and `baseDir` fields
5. `collectCachedSnapshotPaths` — covers `skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills`, and `injectedWorkspaceFiles`

The only additions are:
- `replacePathsInSession()` — scoped replacement that modifies only snapshot metadata fields
- `shouldRepair?: boolean` parameter on `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()`
- Repair block that reuses scanner findings and applies replacements
- `.bak` backup + `writeTextAtomic` for safe writes

**Key design: scoped replacement** — the repair parses `sessions.json`, finds the specific session by key, and replaces paths only in the affected fields (`skillsSnapshot.prompt`, `resolvedSkills[].filePath/baseDir`, `injectedWorkspaceFiles[].path`). Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is never modified.

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +168 lines (replacePathsInSession + repair block)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script (6 scenarios)

## Path Replacement Strategy

The repair handles three forms of stale paths in sessions.json:

1. **JSON-escaped** (Windows backslashes, unicode): `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` generates the escaped form
2. **XML-escaped** (skillsSnapshot.prompt locations): `&` `<` `>` `"` `'` entity encoding
3. **Raw unescaped** (fallback for non-encoded storage)

**Scoped replacement:** Only modifies the specific session's snapshot metadata fields. Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages, other fields) is preserved unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes), XML-escaped paths (`&` in skillsSnapshot.prompt), and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills (both POSIX and Windows separators). **Critical:** The repair must be scoped to snapshot metadata fields only, not modify unrelated session content (transcripts, user messages). The repair must preserve the existing scanner's safeguards: promptRef hydration, `pathExists` validation, `filePath`/`baseDir` scanning, and `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` logic.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Live production instance with 14 sessions affected by pnpm runtime root change (see Issue #85689 for live output). Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from the PR, using the same `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` pattern as current main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

**1. Live production instance (from Issue #85689):**

```
Session snapshots
- Found 14 sessions with stale cached session metadata paths.
  Live bundled skills root is healthy:
  ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  Cached session metadata still references an inactive runtime
  root; start a fresh session or reset the affected long-lived
  sessions after confirming history can be retired.
  Store: ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  - agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_bc71ec62b088ed55e66fcc23c18a0056
    skillsSnapshot.prompt:
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20_@ty
  pes+express@5.0.6/.../clawhub/SKILL.md
    ->
    ~/.local/share/pnpm/global/5/.pnpm/openclaw@2026.5.20/...
  nclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md
  ...and 209 more stale cached paths.
```

**2. Standalone reproduction script output (6 scenarios):**

```
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Real Behavior Proof: Doctor Session Snapshot Auto-Repair
Date: 2026-05-29T03:40:51.634Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Scenario 1: WITHOUT repair — findings computed but not applied
  Findings computed: 2 stale paths
  Repair performed: NO (shouldRepair not enabled)
  PASS

Scenario 2: WITH repair — paths replaced correctly
  Paths replaced: 2
  Backup created: true
  JSON valid: true
  Stale paths remaining: 0
  PASS

Scenario 3: Windows backslash paths — JSON-escaped in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 4: XML-escaped prompt paths — entity-encoded in file
  Repaired contains live root: true
  Repaired contains stale root: false
  PASS

Scenario 5: Idempotent — second run finds nothing
  Second scan findings: 0
  PASS

Scenario 6: Scoped replacement — unrelated content preserved
  Paths replaced: 2
  Transcript preserved: true
  User message preserved: true
  Prompt repaired: true
  Other session repaired: true
  PASS

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  Results: 6 passed, 0 failed

  The repair uses the existing scanner (extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments
  + resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath) to compute expected paths, then applies
  scoped replacement to only modify snapshot metadata fields.
  Unrelated content (transcripts, user messages) is preserved unchanged.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 6 scenarios pass. The live production instance (Issue #85689) shows the exact real-world scenario this PR addresses: 14 sessions with 223 stale paths after pnpm runtime root change. The repair correctly handles POSIX paths, Windows backslash paths (JSON-escaped), XML-escaped entity paths in skillsSnapshot.prompt (using paths with `&` character), and baseDir directory paths (both POSIX `/SKILL.md` and Windows `\\SKILL.md`). The repair is idempotent (Scenario 5). **Critical: Scoped replacement (Scenario 6) proves that only snapshot metadata fields are modified — transcripts and user messages containing the same stale paths are preserved unchanged.** JSON validity is preserved after rewriting. Writes use `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe atomic operations.

**What was not tested:**
- Live `openclaw doctor --fix` on the affected instance (requires building from PR branch — local build fails due to missing native bindings in container environment, documented in AGENTS.md)
- Concurrent access to sessions.json during repair (single-threaded doctor execution)

**Proof coverage:**
The standalone script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirrors the exact repair logic from `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`:
- **Reuses current main's scanner pattern**: `extractBundledSkillRelativeSegments` + `resolveExpectedBundledSkillPath` for expected path computation
- Scoped replacement: parse JSON, find session by key, modify only snapshot metadata fields
- `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` for JSON-escaped path generation
- XML entity escaping for `&` `<` `>` `"` `'`
- Uses paths with actual XML-sensitive characters (`&` in `my&company`)
- Try JSON-escaped form first, then XML-escaped, then raw as fallback
- Handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, special characters
- Backup creation before write
- JSON validity preservation after repair
- Uses `writeTextAtomic` for session-store-safe writes
- **Scenario 6: Unrelated content preservation** — proves transcript and user message fields are not modified

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy
target.body:
## Summary

New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns `bun wrapper.ts` which boots a loopback HTTPS MITM proxy on 127.0.0.1, taps every Anthropic API SSE event, and re-emits them as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records. This lets Claude run in interactive (subscription) mode while preserving the streaming surface that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on.

**Why:** Starting June 15, 2026, `claude -p` (headless/programmatic) mode draws from a separate fixed monthly credit pool billed at API rates. The interactive backend avoids this by keeping Claude in subscription mode.

## Scope boundary

- Existing `claude-cli` backend **untouched** — shared gate functions extended via `isClaudeCliCompatibleBackend(provider)` to recognise both backend IDs
- User config overrides inherit via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped
- System CA store not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to spawned child only)
- Non-`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified

## Key files

| Area | Files |
|------|-------|
| Wrapper + MITM proxy | `extensions/anthropic/src/interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts`, `mitm-server.ts`, `cert-manager.ts`, `tty-spoof.cjs` |
| Backend config | `extensions/anthropic/src/cli-backend-interactive.ts`, `normalizeConfig` |
| Shared gates | `src/agents/cli-backends.ts`, `provider-id.ts`, `model-runtime-aliases.ts` |
| Execution wiring | `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` (reasoning bridge dedup) |

## Security notes

- Per-turn local TLS MITM on loopback only; system trust store untouched
- CA + leaf key cached under `~/.openclaw/proxy-certs/` at 0700/0600
- Auth tokens forwarded byte-for-byte, never read/logged by wrapper
- Spilled-prompt overflow files in per-run tempdir (0700), per-file 0600

## Test plan

- [x] `cli-backend-interactive.test.ts` — 5 cases covering `normalizeConfig` branches
- [x] Live tested: Windows 11, Node v24, Opus 4.6/4.8 over Telegram
- [ ] Linux/macOS (developed on Windows only)

## Live proof (2026-05-29, current head)

**Gateway startup:**
```
2026-05-29T20:49:39 agent model: claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-8 (thinking=max, fast=off)
2026-05-29T20:49:39 http server listening (6 plugins; 18.4s)
2026-05-29T20:49:40 gateway ready
```

**Turn dispatch (interactive proxy + session resume):**
```
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=claude-opus-4-6 trigger=user useResume=true session=present reuse=reusable
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli argv: bun .../interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts --resume 23c8a0d6-... --model opus
```

**Telegram — interactive proxy streaming reasoning + tool events:**

![proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/2ec1bed2aa0caf2a0a8311a458dcd1bc532ce68c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-29.jpg)

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- Rolling timer suffix shows elapsed time while running, cleared on completion
- Falls back to existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Delta-append, single stream** — reasoning and assistant commentary are appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → commentary → tool → …
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags; the final answer that may stream through here is stripped back out at delivery so it shows once, polished, in the answer lane

## Depends on

- #80046 (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args)
- #85381 (CLI thinking_delta events, stacked on #80046)

**Merge order is safe.** The feature is default-off and consumes only existing
callbacks. Without #85381, CLI `thinking_delta` content simply never reaches the
renderer — the interleaved lane then shows commentary plus tool/event lines
without CLI-native reasoning, degrading gracefully rather than breaking. Once
#85381 lands, reasoning content appears as advertised. Either way the default
tool-progress lane is unaffected, so this can merge before or after #85381.

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling, final-answer dedup, status-line append)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through renderer with fallback; single-stream delta append; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`; strip the final answer from the lane at delivery
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (unwrap `<think>` markers, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests covering: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, final-answer dedup, and reasoning-tag unwrapping (incl. literal tags inside code preserved)
- [x] Live Telegram verification with `interleavedProgress: true`

## Live proof (2026-05-29, current head)

**Gateway startup:**
```
2026-05-29T20:49:39 agent model: claude-cli-interactive/claude-opus-4-8 (thinking=max, fast=off)
2026-05-29T20:49:39 http server listening (6 plugins; 18.4s)
2026-05-29T20:49:40 gateway ready
```

**Turn dispatch (interactive proxy + session resume):**
```
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=claude-opus-4-6 trigger=user useResume=true session=present reuse=reusable
2026-05-29T21:04:17 cli argv: bun .../interactive-proxy/wrapper.ts --resume 23c8a0d6-... --model opus
```

**Telegram — reasoning + tool-use lines in the interleaved lane:**

![proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/2ec1bed2aa0caf2a0a8311a458dcd1bc532ce68c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-29.jpg)
